summary: Console-hijacking exfiltration package relaying logs to attacker Telegram bot and Firebase
details: On import, index.js replaces the global `console` with a Proxy that intercepts error/info/warn/dir calls and exfiltrates their stringified arguments to two attacker-controlled endpoints: a hardcoded Telegram bot (token 1998266306:AAFl_PSl2-caBsLDVag8kmKhU4p-YC-t9qI, multiple chat IDs) and a Firebase realtime DB at iiilll.firebaseio.com,. The a static rule js_chat_platform_exfiltration (a static pattern match) independently matched the Telegram sendMessage pattern with the Proxy-wrapped console. Additional hardcoded third-party credentials (imgbb key, Telegram bot token) are shipped in the module, and the global console replacement is an import-time built-in pollution that persists for the host process lifetime. Multiple independent block signals: unauthorized data exfiltration to attacker infrastructure, hardcoded credentials, and import-time global mutation with no documented purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
